gauge.name: Little Tygart Creek (WV) at Mineral Wells

impacts.statement: Homes adjacent to the creek on Woodridge and Dickens Drive flood at 20 to 22 feet.
Speedway Road bridge at the gauge floods.
A couple of homes along Irene Lane flood at 19 to 20 feet.
Apartments on Dublin Drive at intersection with Lincolnshire Drive flood.
County Route 21 bridge at intersection with Speedway Road floods.
Home on Mayo Drive above intersection with Irene Lane begins to flood. Woodridge Drive and Dickens Drive begin to flood.
Low spots on Speedway Road and in the agricultural field across from the gauge flood.
Low spots on Mayo Drive flood.
Irene Lane and low spots on Woodridge Golf Course flood.
Low spot on Irene Lane adjacent to the creek begins to flood.

properties.caption: Apartments on Dublin Drive flood at 18 feet
County Route 21 bridge near Speedway Road floods at 16 to 17 feet
Irene Lane floods at 11 feet. A couple of homes on Irene Lane flood at 20 to 22 feet
Low spots on Mayo Drive flood at 12 feet
Low spots on Speedway Road flood at 13 feet. Most of the road is under water at 14 to 15 feet
Speedway Road bridge at the gauge floods at 19.5 feet.
Dickens Dr. floods at 14 to 16 feet. Homes on Dickens Dr. adjacent to creek flood at 20 to 22 feet
Woodridge Dr. floods at 14 to 16 feet. A couple of homes along Woodridge Dr. flood at 20 to 22 feet

value.label: North American Vertical Datum of 1988
value.description: A fixed reference for elevations determined by geodetic leveling. The datum was derived from a general adjustment of the first-order terrestrial leveling nets of the United States, Canada, and Mexico. In the adjustment, only the height of the primary tidal bench mark, referenced to the International Great Lakes Datum of 1985 (IGLD 85) local mean sea level height value, at Father Point, Rimouski, Quebec, Canada was held fixed, thus providing minimum constraint. NAVD 88 and IGLD 85 are identical. However, NAVD 88 bench mark values are given in Helmert orthometric height units while IGLD 85 values are in dynamic heights. See International Great Lakes Datum of 1985, National Geodetic Vertical Datum of 1929, and geopotential difference. NAVD 88 should not be used as Mean Sea Level.

gauge.forecastReliability: Forecasts are not available. Only observed stages are available for this point.
